title: Fafnir
content:

{{Quote|Your furry friend, ''Fafnir'', must've rounded up every ''snow demon'' in the Polar Wastes, Col. Odan!|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Pursuit">{{StoryCite|story=Pursuit!|book=Star Wars Weekly 60}}</ref>}}
'''Fafnir''' was an [[Akurian]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Akuria II]]. He was apparently second in command to [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Odan]] after the capture of the majority of Odan's forces during an attack on his [[War Sled]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Fafnir, I send you out to save a ''princess'' an' her ''star pilot'' and' ''you'' come back with a pair of clankin' ''mechanicals''!|Odan|<ref name="Treachery" />}}
Operating out of the [[Akuria II]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Akuria Rebel Base|Base]], [[Odan]] ordered Fafnir and a task force of [[Akurian]]s to rescue [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] who were due to arrive on Akuria II for a [[Mission to Akuria II|meeting]] with Odan. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] on the world had decided to set up a ruse in which their [[Odan (impostor)|leader]] would pose as Odan in order to lure the two Rebels into their confidence and get them to spill secrets about the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="Treachery" />

When Fafnir and his men arrived to rescue Skywalker and Organa from this deception, they were treated as enemies due to their fearsome natures. Additionally, because they could not speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], their attempts to rescue the pair only led to more struggle from those they were trying to help. As the Imperials posing as Rebels appeared, Fafnir and his group were forced to retreat, only being able to secure Skywalker's two droids, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]].<ref name="Snow Demons">{{StoryCite|story=The Snow Demons!|book=Pizzazz 11}}</ref> After bringing the droids back to the Rebel base, Fafnir and Odan got into an argument about the failure to rescue the two Rebel leaders. However, Odan finally relented in trying to place blame, and began to formulate a new plan of rescue using Skywalker's droids.<ref name="Treachery" />

Fafnir, Odan, and the two droids boarded a [[snow flyer]] and began to follow the impostor Imperials who had commandeered Odan's [[War Sled]], hoping to catch up with them and stage a rescue. However, the group found the War Sled on a collision course with the [[Geyser Sea]], and after boarding the out of control vehicle, they found that the Imperials had fused the controls and tied Skywalker into one of the seats in the cockpit. With no way to stop the vehicle's suicide run, Fafnir quickly untied the Rebel leader. Directed by Odan he tore open some bulkheads, behind which the Rebels had previously hidden [[power ski]]s. Boarding the power skis the Rebels escaped.<ref name="Snow Fury">{{StoryCite|story=Snow Fury!|book=Pizzazz 14}}</ref> Unfortunately, even though they had escaped one certain death, a huge snow storm had formed in the meantime, putting them all back into immediate danger. To save themselves, Fafnir, Skywalker, and Odan used their blasters to blast a hole in the ground to wait out the storm.<ref name="Ice Worm">{{StoryCite|story=The Ice Worm Cometh!|book=Pizzazz 15}}</ref>

Unfortunately for the stranded group, the hole they made connected them with an [[ice worm]] tunnel, and the entire party fell deep within the planet's crust. However, making good of a bad situation, Fafnir and the rest of the Rebels used their power skis to move quickly through the Ice Worm tunnel in order to catch up with the Imperials who still held Leia Organa captive. As the group moved through the tunnel, they eventually located the Imperials, who had decided to wait out the storm on the surface directly above them. As Skywalker formulated a plan to take their enemy by surprise, a gigantic ice worm appeared and prepared to devour the team. Running to their power skis, Fafnir and the rest of the group were able to divert the Worm's direction by blasting the exhaust from their vehicles into its face. The creature, propelled upward, smashed itself into the parked Imperials above, scattering the entire group.<ref name="Showdown">{{StoryCite|story=Showdown (Pizzazz)|book=Pizzazz 16}}</ref>

As Fafnir and his compatriots climbed up the hole that the Ice Worm had made, the Ice Worm and the remaining [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] fought against each other, eventually wiping each other out. The Rebels surveyed the wreckage of the brief, but brutal struggle; However, they could not locate the Princess anywhere. The Imperial leader had escaped the scene in a Snow Flyer, trying to make his way back to the Imperial's [[Polar Base]]. Luckily, Skywalker was able to catch up to the Snow Flyer on his power ski and rescued the Princess. With the Imperial leader captured, the Rebels decided to implicate a ruse of their own, and they forced the Imperial to give them the access codes to enter the Polar Base.<ref name="Pursuit" />

As Odan and the other Rebels used the access codes to destroy the gate into the Imperial garrison, Fafnir gathered the rest of his Akurians and moved to attack the base. Once the energy gate had been disabled, the Akurians rushed into the opening. With help from Skywalker and Organa who had commandeered [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]], Fafnir and his people were able to defeat the Imperials and end the Imperial presence on their world.<ref name="Pursuit" />

[[File:Attack_on_polar_base.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Fafnir's army of Akurians attack the Imperial Polar Base]]

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|The Imperials have hunted 'em like ''animals'' for years. They've been itchin' to ''strike back''--|Odan on Fafnir and the Akurians|<ref name="Pursuit" />}}
At first glance Fafnir appeared to be quiet and obedient, doing whatever he was told by Odan. However, this was merely because of Fafnir's dedication to the Akuria II Rebels. If the bombastic Odan ever became too harsh with Fafnir, Fafnir would reveal his anger with a growl, hinting at the depths beneath his quiet exterior.<ref name="Treachery" />

Like many other Akurians, Fafnir had been abused and hunted at the hands of the Empire. When the defenses of the Imperial Polar Base had been breached, Fafnir ceased to be the quiet sidekick, and commanded an army of his fellow Akurians to invade and conquer the base.<ref name="Pursuit" />

[[File:Fafnir_equipped.jpg|thumb|right|Fafnir equipped with his bandolier and blaster rifle]]

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|Rip open the bulkhead panels, Fafnir... we hid ''power skis'' there for emergencies!|Odan|<ref name="Snow Fury" />}}
Fafnir, like most Akurians, was big and strong, able to rip open bulkheads with little difficulty.<ref name="Snow Fury" /> His shaggy fur protected him from the cold of the Akurian [[Polar Wastes]], granting him yet another advantage over the [[Human/Legends|humans]] in their [[thermal suit]]s.<ref name="Snow Demons" />

Despite being unable to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], Fafnir grasped technology very well. He was adept at piloting vehicles like power skis, which he could do one-handed while carrying a heavy load,<ref name="Snow Fury" /> and skilled with a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]].<ref name="Ice Worm" />

==Equipment==
Fafnir frequently wore a [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]] and [[Bandolier/Legends|bandolier]], and carried a blaster rifle.<ref name="Snow Fury" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Wikipedia:Fafnir|Fafnir]] is a character from the Norse mythology.
